description: WritableSecretStore contract states 'when put returns Ok(()), the value is durably stored' (secretx-core/src/lib.rs:1268-1270). secretx-file/src/lib.rs:165-184 writes the temp file via OpenOptions+write_all, then std::fs::rename, with no fsync(2) on either the file fd or the parent directory. POSIX rename(2) is atomic for crash-safety on the inode swap, but does NOT imply the file's data has reached durable storage; on a power loss between write_all and the next periodic flush, post-recovery the renamed file can contain garbage, zeros, or an old directory entry pointing at unallocated blocks. Fix: f.sync_all() before drop, then File::open(parent).and_then(|d| d.sync_all()) after rename. Reproduction: write secret, kill -9 the host, examine post-reboot file contents.
title: [bug] put() does not fsync — durability contract violated across crashes
